summary: Set 086 Session 2 (set-terminal: diagnostics, onboarding, legibility). FOUR deliverables + release-prep. (1) Togglable Copilot-CLI transport diagnostics: new ai_router/transport_diagnostics.py -- on a FAILED copilot-cli dispatch on the route()/verify() path, emit a structured JSONL record (error_class, exit_code, argv with the -p prompt REDACTED, auth-reprobe result, capped+prompt-scrubbed stderr tail) to a config/env-gated log (transports.copilot-cli.diagnostics.{enabled,log_path}; env DABBLER_COPILOT_DIAGNOSTICS / _LOG override both ways), and embed a compact prompt-free summary in the raised CopilotCliRoutingError (generator path) AND the verification_unavailable reason (verifier path) regardless of the toggle. Wired at the single dispatch choke point _copilot_cli_dispatch(role=...); the transport stays pure; the write is best-effort and never masks the transport failure (build/serialize/write all under broad guards; malformed metadata / non-str stderr coerced). (2) Reader-legibility guardrail (extension): tierLegibility.isRecognizedVerdictToken + verdictFractionTooltip flag an unrecognized/confabulated token (manual-override-development) instead of rendering it as a clean verdict; the workflow-state ladder already treated only VERIFIED as verified, so the tooltip was the one laundering channel. (3) Setup-checklist onboarding wiring: docs/copilot-seat-setup-checklist.md refined against S1 (preflight now exists; verdict allowlist now enforced) and linked from quick-start, tier-model, README, both consumer-bootstrap templates (+ regenerated cold-start goldens + dist bundle), and the form's missing-CLI warning; the preflight remediation already referenced it (S1). (4) Verdict-contract + Copilot-seat-verification-path docs: corrected the stale session-state-schema.md / disposition-schema.md prose to the S1-shipped exact-allowlist reality (L-064-8), documented the Set-086 integrity guards in tier-model.md; the JSON schema note was already current from S1. Release-prep: ext 0.41.0 + router 0.31.0 bumped with Unreleased CHANGELOG entries; a candidate 0.41.0 test .vsix built for operator-laptop evidence (Marketplace/PyPI publish stays operator-gated, pending). Also (operator-directed mid-session): added the severity-gated verification-stop rule to the constitution's Recovery-and-escalation section (automatic rounds hard-capped at two; no Critical/Major past the cap -> suspend as effectively-VERIFIED; a disputed Critical/Major -> third-provider opinion or operator adjudication). Suites: pytest 2872 passed / 5 skipped; extension unit 1273 passing; drift-guard 25 green. Verification (mandatory cross-provider, no confabulation): FOUR real routed rounds via gpt-5-4 (anthropic auto-excluded as orchestrator provider; both providers were transiently 429 earlier and cleared). R1 (1 blocking): diagnostics wiring lacked integration tests + emit only caught OSError -> FIXED (integration tests at the __init__ call sites + broadened exception handling). R2 (3 findings): stderr could leak the -p prompt / verifier-path lacked the summary / reprobe under-reported -> all FIXED + tested. R3 (2 findings): helpers could raise on malformed metadata -> FIXED; reader guardrail prefix-match admits VERIFIED_NOT_REALLY -> operator-adjudicated MINOR/will-not-fix (the S1 writer already rejects such look-alikes at write time, so they cannot reach disk via a blessed writer). R4 (1 finding, verifier-labeled Major, orchestrator-disputed + operator-adjudicated MINOR): prompt-scrub does not cover CRLF/escaped/bytes-repr stderr variants -- a new PERMUTATION of the already-settled R2 prompt-scrub class (edge-case exhaustion). Per the operator's severity-gated-stop rule and explicit directive to wrap up, the loop was SUSPENDED after R4: every substantive finding is fixed; the residual is immaterial defense-in-depth on an unlikely vector. See verification_round_provenance + close_status below.

close_status.explanation: All Session 2 work is complete, tested (pytest 2872/5, extension 1273, drift 25 green), and genuinely verified across FOUR real cross-provider routed rounds -- the confabulation risk this set exists to prevent is absent. Every substantive finding is fixed; the residual (R3 reader-guardrail prefix-match; R4 prompt-scrub of escaped/CRLF/bytes-repr stderr variants) is operator-adjudicated MINOR / edge-case-exhaustion and will-not-fix. The formal close_session state-flip is DEFERRED: this is a limited-budget repo (budget.yaml threshold_usd=10, NOT zero-budget), so --manual-verify / manual-via-other-engine is illegal and --force still runs the backstop; the ONLY machine-legal close is a non-blocking routed verdict, and gpt-5-4 returned a fresh UNRATED nit every round (4x) which the gate treats as blocking. That is exactly the gap the new severity-gated-stop rule targets but which is NOT yet machine-enforced (the operator directed deferring rule-enforcement to focus on Set 087). Resolution options for the formal close, operator's choice: (a) enforce the rule in close_backstop/is_blocking so unrated findings past the round cap are non-blocking, then close settles on the ISSUES_FOUND claim; (b) a single round on a different verifier (gemini-pro / gpt-5-5, now registered) that returns clean; (c) a brief operator budget-tier toggle to attest a manual close. Work is committed + pushed so nothing is lost and Set 087 is unblocked.

verification_round_provenance.rounds: R1 gpt-5-4 (1 blocking: integration-test gap + OSError-only guard; FIXED). R2 gpt-5-4 (3: stderr prompt-leak / verifier-path summary parity / reprobe under-report; all FIXED). R3 gpt-5-4 (2: malformed-metadata guard FIXED; reader prefix-match ADJUDICATED minor). R4 gpt-5-4 (1, verifier-labeled Major, disputed+adjudicated minor: prompt-scrub escaped/CRLF variants -- a permutation of the settled R2 class, edge-case exhaustion).
verification_round_provenance.cross_provider_guarantee: Held every round: verifier provider (openai/gpt-5-4) != orchestrator effective provider (anthropic, auto-excluded).
verification_round_provenance.stop_rationale: Suspended after R4 per the operator's severity-gated-stop rule (now in the constitution): automatic rounds hard-capped at two; no material Critical/Major remained past the cap (R3/R4 findings are orchestrator-disputed, operator-adjudicated minor, and R4 is a permutation of an already-settled class); grinding further rounds burns money/time for no correctness gain. Bounded-round discipline was violated by running R3/R4 before the rule was written -- that error is what prompted codifying the rule this session.
